topic: Two colleagues are both considered for the same promotion
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the office hummed, a grating soundtrack to my mounting anxiety. I watched Sarah across the cubicle partition as she cheerfully accepted the congratulations from Mr. Henderson. Her smile was effortless, a bright beacon in the muted landscape of my own workspace. My hands, gripping the edge of my desk, were slick with a sudden, cold sweat. I replayed the interview in my mind, every stammer, every moment where I faltered, looping over and over. When the manager finally called my name, the sound felt distant, muffled. I just nodded, a jerky, mechanical movement, and retreated to my chair, the silence of my desk suddenly deafening.

[story 2]

I kept catching my reflection in the darkened monitor screen. My face felt hot, flushed beneath the harsh office lighting. Every time I glanced at the framed commendations on my wall, they seemed to mock me—proof of competence that felt utterly undeserved. When Mark announced he was the chosen one, the air seemed to thin out around me. I felt a physical tightening in my chest, a knot that refused to loosen, making each breath shallow. I avoided eye contact with everyone, focusing instead on meticulously organizing a stack of obsolete files, my movements stiff and overly deliberate. I wished the floor would simply swallow me whole.

[story 3]

The celebratory coffee break was torture. Everyone clustered around Sarah, their laughter echoing in the open-plan area, a sound that felt miles away from my own isolation. I nursed my lukewarm mug, the ceramic warm against my palms, trying to project an air of calm that my insides were actively fighting against. When the discussion turned to the promotion details, my throat constricted. I managed a weak, "Congratulations, Sarah," the words tasting like ash. I stood up too quickly, the chair scraping loudly against the linoleum, and fled toward the relative anonymity of the breakroom, needing to escape the palpable scrutiny of the room.
